methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OSH Cut, a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Chamberlain, presents a valuation challenge due to its nascent funding stage and the structural limitations of comparable company analysis in this sector. The robotics and manufacturing universe contains 9 identifiable peers—1 public (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private entities including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics—yet none provide a direct methodological anchor for valuation.

The peer-median approach is not applicable here. Of the 11 same-sector peers identified, zero qualify as matching same-stage, zero as adjacent-stage, and zero as similar-valuation-tier comparables. This filtering yielded insufficient coverage (the threshold requires a minimum of 3 datapoints) to support a credible median multiple. An all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models, capital intensity, and commercialization timelines across the broader robotics landscape.

Given the data constraints, traditional triangulation methods cannot be reliably deployed. The valuation range cannot be supported by quantitative peer analysis at this time. Key uncertainties include the absence of financial performance metrics (revenue, burn rate, unit economics) and the wide dispersion in stage and maturity among available comparables. A credible valuation would require either: (i) disclosure of OSH Cut's own financials to anchor a revenue or growth multiple, or (ii) identification of additional same-stage private robotics peers with comparable capital deployment profiles.
